title: Classifying Quality (Textbook Quality)
concept: Classifier models are trained to score documents based on how closely they match educational or reference text, selecting 'textbook-quality' pages for training.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using a 'textbook quality' classifier?

options.text: To filter out messy web pages and keep clear, educational ones.
To buy school textbooks online for cheap.

exercises.explain: A quality classifier helps filter out messy internet clutter so the AI learns from clean, structured, and educational writing.

cards.title: Sorting the Web
cards.body: The internet is full of amazing facts, but it's also full of messy chat rooms, advertisements, and spam. How do AI researchers find the absolute best, clearest writing to teach their models? They train a digital gatekeeper to find 'textbook-quality' pages!

cards.title: What is a Quality Classifier?
cards.body: A Quality Classifier is a specialized AI helper trained to read web pages and grade them. It gives high scores to documents that are written clearly, educationally, and professionally—just like a school textbook—and filters out messy or useless pages.

cards.title: The School Librarian
cards.body: Imagine you are sorting books for a school library. You have a giant pile of papers: some are messy personal diaries, others are spam flyers, and some are clear school textbooks. A Quality Classifier acts like a super-fast librarian who looks at each paper and asks, 'Does this explain things clearly like a textbook?' If yes, it goes into the library pile!

cards.title: Textbook vs. Trash
cards.body:
Let's look at two pages about space:
• Page A: 'OMG space is so cool! aliens are real check out my store link below!!!'
• Page B: 'Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun. It is a cold, dusty desert world.'

The classifier scores Page A very low (web spam) and Page B very high (textbook-quality).

cards.title: Myth: They check for Truth
cards.body: People think these classifiers check if information is 100% correct. Actually, they check style, not facts! A classifier looks for clear explanations, organized headers, and good grammar, rather than checking a database to verify truths.

cards.title: The Quality Filter
cards.body: See how the classifier splits the wild internet into useful training data versus ignored noise based on writing style.

left.text: Structured guides, clear explanations, correct grammar, encyclopedias.

right.text: Clickbait ads, keyboard smashes, repetitive spam, broken links.

exercises.prompt: Why do we want to select 'textbook-quality' web pages to train AI models?

options.text: To train the AI on clear, educational, and structured language.
To make sure the AI knows how to write internet spam.
To block human users from reading school textbooks online.
To translate websites into different languages.

exercises.explain: We want AI to learn from clear, structured, and informative writing, so we train it using educational and reference-style documents.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A Quality Classifier deletes low-quality websites from the actual internet permanently.
exercises.explain: Classifiers grade pages on a scale of quality; they don't just delete them. Low-scoring pages are simply filtered out of the final training set.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the sentence about training data quality.
exercises.sentence: AI models use classifiers to find high-quality, __________-like pages from the messy internet.
exercises.explain: Classifier models search for 'textbook' quality writing because it contains clear explanations and structured logic, which are great for training AI.

exercises.prompt: Which of these three sentences would a Textbook Quality Classifier score the HIGHEST?

options.text: 'i think photosynthesis is like plants eating sunshine or something lol'
'Buy cheap plant fertilizer here! 50% off code: GREEN!'
'Photosynthesis is the biological process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy.'

exercises.explain: The third choice is written in a helpful, educational style (defining a term clearly), which is what a textbook classifier looks for. The others are just informal thoughts or ads.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes what a 'Textbook Quality' Classifier actually does when evaluating web pages?

options.text: It fact-checks every sentence to make sure it is 100% true.
It looks at writing style and structure to see if it reads like educational material.
It deletes any page that is shorter than five paragraphs.
It translates low-quality pages into different languages automatically.

exercises.explain: Quality Classifiers do not check if facts are true; they only evaluate if the writing style, structure, and language look like educational or reference material. Fact-checking is a separate, more complex task!
